Yan Jun (Mancai)
嚴畯 (曼才)
Lived ADca.165–ca.242
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
byZL181
onOctober 10th, 2025

Yan Jun, styledMancai, was fromPengcheng[彭城].
From youth, he was engrossed in studies. He was adept at theBook of Odes,Book of Documents, and the Three Rites, and he was good at theExplanation of Characters.
He evaded chaos to theJiangdong region[江東]. He,Zhuge Jin, andBu Zhiwere of equal fame and amicable with each other. By nature, he was simple, honest, pure, and generous. With this being in relation to people, he spoke loyally and advised well. He had the aspiration of offering assistance and being beneficial.
Zhang Zhaorecommended him toSun Quan.[Sun] Quanappointed him to serve asCavalry CommandantandGentleman Attendant. When theGeneral who Stretches Across the River,Lu Su, died,[Sun] Quanhad[Yan] Junreplace[Lu] Su, supervise ten thousand soldiers, and garrison atLukou. The masses all believed that[Yan] Junwas good.[Yan] Junwould make several firm resignations, saying, “I have always been a bookish scholar, lacking experience in military matters. With me holding this in spite of my lack of talent, this is sure to culminate in regrettable misfortune.” He spoke his words with conviction to the point of shedding tears.A[Sun] Quanwould heed him... (truncated)
